title: Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as US Intelligence Chief
subtitle: The Director of National Intelligence steps down to support her husband during a health crisis.

summary.en: Tulsi Gabbard has announced her resignation as the US Director of National Intelligence. She plans to leave her post on June 30 to care for her husband, who was recently diagnosed with bone cancer. President Donald Trump expressed his support for her decision and praised her work. Her time in office was marked by debates over foreign policy and intelligence reports. Aaron Lukas will serve as the acting director following her departure.
summary.zh: 美國國家情報總監圖爾西·加巴德宣布辭職。她計畫於六月三十日離職，以照顧近期確診骨癌的丈夫。川普總統對她的決定表示支持，並肯定了她的工作表現。加巴德的任期以關於外交政策與情報報告的爭論為特徵。在她離職後，亞倫·盧卡斯將擔任代理總監。

paragraphs.en: Tulsi Gabbard, the Director of National Intelligence for the United States, has announced that she will resign from her position. In a letter to President Donald Trump, Gabbard explained that her husband, Abraham, was recently diagnosed with a rare form of bone cancer. She stated that she needs to step away from her demanding role to support him through his treatment. Her resignation is set to take effect on June 30.
paragraphs.zh: 美國國家情報總監圖爾西·加巴德（Tulsi Gabbard）宣布將辭去職務。加巴德在致川普總統的信中解釋，她的丈夫亞伯拉罕（Abraham）近期被診斷出罹患一種罕見的骨癌。她表示，為了陪伴丈夫接受治療，她必須卸下這份高壓的工作。她的辭職將於六月三十日正式生效。

paragraphs.en: President Trump shared his reaction on social media, stating that Gabbard has done an "incredible job" and that she will be missed. He also noted that he understands her need to be with her husband during this difficult time. To fill the vacancy, the president announced that Aaron Lukas, who currently serves as the principal deputy director, will take over as the acting director of national intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 川普總統在社群媒體上回應，稱讚加巴德表現「極為出色」，並表示會懷念與她共事的時光。他也提到，他理解她在這段艱難時期陪伴丈夫的需求。為了填補職位空缺，總統宣布現任首席副總監亞倫·盧卡斯（Aaron Lukas）將接任代理國家情報總監。

paragraphs.en: Gabbard’s time in the administration was notable for several reasons. A former Democratic congresswoman from Hawaii and a military veteran, she was a strong supporter of Donald Trump during his 2024 presidential campaign. After Trump returned to the White House in 2025, she was confirmed as one of the most powerful figures in the American intelligence community. However, her tenure was not without challenges. She faced scrutiny regarding her past political positions and her views on international conflicts.
paragraphs.zh: 加巴德在政府任職期間的表現備受關注，原因有幾點。身為前夏威夷州民主黨籍聯邦眾議員及退伍軍人，她在二零二四年的總統大選中大力支持川普。二零二五年川普重返白宮後，她被任命為美國情報界最具權勢的人物之一。然而，她的任期並非一帆風順，過去的政治立場以及對國際衝突的看法，都曾讓她面臨外界的嚴格檢視。

paragraphs.en: One major point of tension involved the conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. Gabbard, who has long been known for her opposition to foreign wars, often found herself in a difficult position as the administration took military action. In March, during a congressional hearing, she was questioned about whether the White House knew about the potential risks of the conflict. At the time, she avoided giving a direct answer, which led to criticism from some lawmakers.
paragraphs.zh: 其中一個主要的緊張點涉及美國、以色列與伊朗之間的衝突。長期以來以反對海外戰爭立場著稱的加巴德，在政府採取軍事行動時，經常陷入尷尬處境。三月時，她在國會聽證會上被問及白宮是否知曉衝突的潛在風險。當時她並未給予正面回應，這也引發了部分國會議員的批評。

paragraphs.en: Furthermore, there were public disagreements between Gabbard and the president regarding Iran’s nuclear program. While Gabbard told Congress that the intelligence community did not believe Iran was building a nuclear weapon, President Trump publicly disagreed with her assessment. He stated that he believed Iran was very close to having such a weapon, and when asked about the difference in their statements, he simply told reporters that she was "wrong."
paragraphs.zh: 此外，加巴德與總統在伊朗核子計畫議題上也曾公開意見不合。儘管加巴德向國會表示，情報界並不認為伊朗正在製造核子武器，但川普總統公開反對她的評估。他表示相信伊朗距離擁有核武已非常接近，當被問及兩人說法不一致時，他僅對記者表示她「錯了」。

paragraphs.en: Despite these disagreements, Gabbard’s departure is part of a larger trend within the Trump administration. She is the fourth Cabinet member to leave her post this year. Other high-profile departures include Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and Attorney General Pam Bondi. These changes have led to questions about the stability of the administration as it continues to manage ongoing international and domestic issues.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在這些分歧，加巴德的離職仍是川普政府內部更大趨勢的一部分。她是今年第四位離職的內閣成員。其他備受矚目的離職官員還包括國土安全部長克莉絲蒂·諾姆（Kristi Noem）與司法部長潘·邦迪（Pam Bondi）。這些人事異動引發了外界對於政府在持續處理國內外議題時，其穩定性是否受到影響的質疑。

paragraphs.en: In her resignation letter, Gabbard expressed gratitude for the trust the president placed in her over the last year and a half. She also mentioned that she is committed to ensuring a smooth transition for her successor. Although she faced criticism during her time in office, she maintained that she worked to bring more transparency and integrity to the intelligence community. As she prepares to leave, her focus remains entirely on her family and her husband’s health battle.
paragraphs.zh: 在辭職信中，加巴德對總統在過去一年半給予的信任表達了感謝。她也提到，她致力於確保繼任者的交接過程順利。儘管在任期間面臨批評，她仍堅持自己致力於為情報界帶來更高的透明度與誠信。隨著離職日期臨近，她目前的重心完全放在家庭以及丈夫對抗病魔的過程上。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To leave a job or position, usually temporarily or for personal reasons.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是「走開」，這裡指辭去職務。文中：She stated that she needs to step away from her demanding role to support him through his treatment.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To assume control or responsibility for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指接任職位。文中：To fill the vacancy, the president announced that Aaron Lukas, who currently serves as the principal deputy director, will take over as the acting director of national intelligence.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A job or position, especially one in an organization or government.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（郵寄）或名詞（貼文），這裡指工作職位。文中：She is the fourth Cabinet member to leave her post this year.

sources.title: US: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's intelligence chief

quiz.question: What is the official reason provided for Tulsi Gabbard's resignation?

options.A: She wants to pursue a new career in the private sector.
options.B: She needs to care for her husband who is battling cancer.
options.C: She disagrees with the President's foreign policy on Iran.
options.D: She is being replaced due to criticism from lawmakers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，Gabbard 在給總統的信中明確提到，她的丈夫 Abraham 最近被診斷出患有罕見的骨癌，因此她需要辭職以陪伴並支持他進行治療。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 雖然文章後段有提到她與總統在伊朗議題上有分歧，但這並非她辭職的官方理由；選項 D 雖然提到她曾面臨批評，但文章並未指出這是她離職的主因。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the relationship between Gabbard and President Trump?

options.A: They had a strictly professional relationship with no personal respect.
options.B: The President was unhappy with her performance and forced her to resign.
options.C: They maintained a respectful working relationship despite significant policy disagreements.
options.D: The President fully agreed with all of Gabbard's intelligence assessments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。從文章第二段可以看出，總統公開稱讚她做得「令人難以置信」（incredible job），並表示會想念她；同時，第四與第五段提到他們在伊朗核計畫及戰爭議題上有公開的分歧，甚至總統曾說她「錯了」。綜合這些資訊，可以推論儘管政策觀點不同，雙方仍維持了基本的尊重與專業互動。選項 A 與事實不符；選項 B 缺乏證據，總統的公開發言顯示他支持她的決定；選項 D 明顯與第五段內容矛盾。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the word 'tenure' mean in the context of Gabbard's time in office?

options.A: The physical building where she worked.
options.B: The specific skills she used to perform her job.
options.C: The period of time someone holds a particular position.
options.D: The salary she received for her government service.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在文中「However, her tenure was not without challenges」這句話裡，tenure 指的是她擔任國家情報總監的那段任期或職位期間。選項 A 將其誤解為實體辦公地點；選項 B 誤將其解釋為能力或技巧；選項 D 則錯誤地將其與薪資掛鉤。根據上下文，這是一個關於時間跨度的詞彙，用來描述她在該職位上經歷的挑戰。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main focus of the article?

options.A: A detailed analysis of the U.S. intelligence community's failures regarding Iran.
options.B: A report on the personal life and medical history of Abraham Gabbard.
options.C: A discussion on why the Trump administration is failing to keep staff members.
options.D: An overview of Tulsi Gabbard's resignation, her tenure, and the political context surrounding it.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。這篇文章開頭報導了 Gabbard 的辭職消息，接著回顧了她任內的政治背景、與總統的政策分歧，並將其辭職放入內閣變動的更大趨勢中討論，最後提到她對未來的承諾。選項 A 僅涵蓋文章的一小部分；選項 B 過於聚焦個人細節而非報導重點；選項 C 雖有提及內閣變動，但這只是文章的一部分，而非全文主旨。選項 D 最能完整涵蓋報導的廣度。
